name: Chicken Stock
recipeIngredient: 1 leek, peeled and cut into medium pieces
2 celery stalks, cut into medium pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Create a bouquet garni using the two leek tops. Place your favorite mild herbs (fresh parsley is recommended) on one piece of the leek top, cover with another piece, and tie together.
2. In a large soup pot, heat oil over medium heat. Add diced carrot, leek, and celery, and stir until softened but not browned.
3. Add chicken bones and cook for 1-2 minutes. Pour in white vermouth and bring to a boil.
4. When the vermouth starts boiling, add water until the chicken bones and vegetables are completely covered. Return to a boil.
5. Lower heat and skim off foam with a ladle as needed. Simmer for 10 minutes.
6. Add bouquet garni and onion. Continue simmering for an additional 2-2.5 hours, replenishing water if necessary.
7. Line a strainer with cheesecloth over a large bowl. Ladle stock into the cheesecloth to strain out vegetables and bones. Press bones with ladle to extract maximum flavor.
8. For a stronger stock, boil it down until reduced to half its original volume.
